reasons: Valir can push Terizla away before Terizla reaches the center of the fight.
Terizla's damage is strongest when enemies stay near him, so Valir's spacing tools reduce his main value.

proof.summary: Terizla wants close-range AoE trades, while Valir can knock him back, slow him, and force him to spend time walking instead of hitting.
worksBestWhen: Valir keeps distance and saves knockback for Terizla's commit.
Allies avoid grouping inside Terizla's control area.
failureCases: Terizla catches Valir from fog or after Valir wastes knockback.
Valir's team fights in a tight choke where Terizla can still land AoE.

reasons: Terizla's durability makes him a good target for Karrie's repeated max-HP true-damage pattern.
Karrie can punish Terizla if he is forced to walk through frontline space without reaching her.

proof.summary: Terizla wants to survive through incoming damage while swinging in melee range, while Karrie's Lightwheel Mark punishes high-HP durable targets.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has peel and can attack from safe range.
Terizla is forced to walk through the frontline before reaching Karrie.
failureCases: Terizla lands his control on Karrie.
Karrie is caught before stacking Lightwheel Mark.

reasons: Lunox threatens Terizla with target-HP scaling magic damage.
She can punish Terizla's low mobility if she keeps enough spacing and avoids being pulled.

proof.summary: Terizla's strength is durability in close fights, while Lunox can chip and burst him with Chaos Assault before he fully controls the fight.
worksBestWhen: Lunox plays outside Terizla's engage range.
Terizla has already used his main control or is forced to walk forward.
failureCases: Lunox is caught by Terizla's ultimate.
Lunox uses defensive timing too early and cannot finish the trade.

reasons: Diggie can reduce the value of Terizla's teamfight control when Terizla tries to pull multiple allies.
This is more of a teamfight counter than a lane counter.

proof.summary: Terizla wants to trap enemies in his AoE control, while Diggie can protect nearby allies with shield and control immunity during the engage window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves Time Journey for Terizla's real teamfight engage.
Teammates stay close enough to receive Diggie's protection.
failureCases: Terizla baits Time Journey before committing.
Terizla catches Diggie first or fights while Diggie's ultimate is unavailable.

reasons: Pharsa can damage Terizla from outside his preferred melee range.
Terizla struggles when forced to walk into long-range poke before he can start the fight.

proof.summary: Terizla wants enemies close enough for AoE swings and control, while Pharsa can use long-range artillery to pressure him before he reaches the fight.
worksBestWhen: Pharsa positions behind vision and frontline.
failureCases: Terizla reaches Pharsa through flank or fog.
Pharsa channels too close and gets interrupted.
